reviews.review_text: Nice and affordable park with excellent service- big ups to the blonde girl with curly hair especially for being so friendly and helpful!
Nice motel rooms, great staff and lots of amenities.
Clean rooms happy friendly staff tidy grounds great place

Very short stay but lived every single moment...Very warm weather and the pool was like a bowl of hot soup but the kids loved it and played for hours in it!!!!
Pet friendly, bbq everywhere and the kitchen is fully equipped!!!
It is ok and large spot,, the security code to go anywhere is exhausting

Guter Campground mit zwei Poolanlagen und mehreren Toilettenhäusern.
Nebensaison ist der Park nicht so voll. Ist von Vorteil. Die hauptküche und Pool sind gross. Jeh nach dem wo man platziert wird hat man moderne Ausstattung, oder halt in die Jahregekommene Toiletten und Duschen.

Alles in allem ein toller Ort um zu übernachten für paar Tage.

Groot caravanpark, zeer commercieel en duur. 72,50 dollar voor een powerplaats met weinig schaduw. Sanitair is ook groot, schoon en netjes.
Gelijk bij binnenkomst een verkoopster t.a.v. de dolfijnen te zien hoe ze gevoerd worden want zelf voeren mag niet meer.  Betaal he dus 15dollar op voor.
Afijn we zijn hier maar 1x…

Exmouth is great, and the services offered by the Park are also fine. However, we had a private house (kitchen, living room, bedroom and bathroom) in the park. Unfortunately, it was infested by cockroaches. We found roughly 15 ones in different sizes just the first day.
Good park but for the price you pay i think all the kids shouldn't have to pay for the games in the games room? Thats just poor and a money grab. Also who has coins 🪙 🤔 👀 😳
Very nice campsite and, above all, clean. Recommended stop!

Booked originally for 2 nights but ended up staying for 7 - this is how good this place is!! The site is absolutely brilliant great facilities like the camp kitchens and BBQ areas, spotlessly clean ablution blocks , immaculate grounds and grass by the workers with moving the sprinklers throughout the day.Even the washing machines for $5 are brilliant value and the washing lines to dry your clothes . Both pools were brilliant and clean too ... If your in the area this is the place to stay !! Some amazing beaches and attractions in the area also.
Nice place for family

Good facilities at this park. Lovely pool space and proximity to town.
For travellers who need to peg gazebos or tents, concrete cement ground is not suitable for this unless you bring a drill and drilling pegs.

Witnessed a neighbour who brought a tent being denied a drill, therefore he was unable to pitch his tent due to being on an unpowered site. This should be reflected by the nightly price.

This place has a number of cabins but its mostly for campers and caravans im in a 4x4 with a roof top tent.
Facilities are great with a pool jacquizzi, games room, huge kitchen toilets and showers cleaned regularly laundry with tumble dryers ($5 for each machine you use)
Also has plenty of BBQ's and picnic benches a bargain for only $53 one night for me
Prices obviously vary depending on the space you need or if you have a cabin.
Caravans and tents can choose to have a space with or without an electric point, id dwfinitely use RAC resorts in future if i need to.

Sehr schöner und sauberer Platz. Das Personal war freundlich. Wir hatten einen Platz mit Naturgrund, was uns sehr gefallen hat. Es gibt auch auf Steinboden, mit oder ohne eigenes WC/Dusche und eine sehr grosse, saubere Campingküche wie einen aussen BBQ Bereich. Duschen/WC waren sauber. Was ich jedoch noch immer nicht verstehe ist, warum Frauen ihre Haare nach dem Kämmen auf den Boden werfen, statt in den Kübel. Selbst wenn man mit dem Mop den Boden nach dem Duschen wischt, ist er nachher voller Haare. Ekelhaft. Hier wäre ein Schaber besser. Und Frauen, hört mit diesem Unsinn auf und kämmt eure Haare an einem Ort wo ihr sie wegwerfen könnt. Eltern, bringt das euren Kindern bei.

Expensive
No unpowerd sites for campervans only tents
Out of season and out of school holidays ( not even peak season )
I guess more aimed at families with kids
Definitely would not become a member of RAC
Great camp kitchens
Disgustingly dirty and mouldy ablutions

Booked and paid for a caravan slab site in person two weeks before, only to be informed on our return by the assistant manager that a slab site which we paid for in advance was now not available. Her demeaning and belittling manner and attitude left a lot to be desired. Perhaps she needs a holiday. We won’t be back. Park itself was well presented. Top vote for pool, games room and campers kitchen. New amenities awesome. Rock hard compacted dirt sites not the best feature. Little to no shade.

Great caravan park.
Generous sites
Lots of facilities, all clean
Great location
We loved our time here

We recently stayed here for 3 weeks in our caravan.  The site's are large enough. We stayed during the September school holidays.  I felt sorry for the cleaning staff as the bathrooms during this period were disgusting 1st thing in the morning.  I even saw faeces on a toilet seat one morning.  Huge piles of toilet paper strewn over the floors.  There probably needs to be some kind of emphasis on the rules for the facilities during these periods. The cleaning staff had a tough job to do. As soon as the school holidays ended the facilities became spotless again. I have no problems with the staff at all. This just needs to be addressed so that everyone can enjoy their time here.
Always good to stop in, recharge the batteries, do the washing, meet the local emus! Walk to the whalebone for lunch!
Large site for our 20ft caravan. Well situated in Exy. Nice pools and clean facilities
Siamo stati al campground, ordinato, pulito e con servizi di ottima qualitá
If you tow a large caravan with a large tug then you will be happy here. Some big sites.
아이들과 지내기좋은 곳이었어요. 수영장과 게임룸은 좋았어요. 식당도 크고 이용하기 편했어요. 걸어서 갈만한 식당들도 있고 차를 이용해 케이프레인지 국립공원 방문도 좋았어요. 다만 어제(2025년 10월 12일 기준) 우리의 옆사이트(17번)에서 체크인후 계속 담배를 펴서 너무 괴로웠어요. 아이들도 많은 캠핑장에서 무례한 행동덕에 6박 마무리가 슬펐어요.

Big bays
Clean toilets

We stayed on an unpowered site. Don’t book this if you want a specious, quiet camp spot which is not in the front of the entrance..! We did ask to move further into the park, unfortunately the staff couldn’t help us since the very little amount of unpowered sites were all fully booked

Nice place to stay, clean common areas and amenities, staff were helpful and friendly.
Nice big caravan sites.
Only downside would be the ground being so difficult to peg awning or shade walls down (even with an impact driver drill and metal screw pegs)
Spacious sites, clean and tidy amenities, friendly staff, great central point for exploring this areas immensely diverse coastline

Positives - Location, great camp kitchen, 2 swimming pools, large clean showers and toilets, friendly staff
Negatives - Expensive, lots of sites have no shade, 2 pools but both over run with noisy children. 1 pool should be adults only.
Beautiful and large camp ground. Ablution blocks very clean and spacious.  Friendly staff. Visiting Emus was awesome 👌
Terrific facilities, well-maintained and clean. Great staff. Seamless booking and check-in process. A great place to set up base as a solo tent camper, any sized camping set up or family holiday.

We stayed there for two nights on powered sites. We couldn't book two consecutive nights on one site, so we made two separate bookings. The first night on site 144 was quite pleasant, but on the second day we had to move to a pitch right next to the amenities block. Unfortunately, it was busy until late in the evening and into the night, and every time someone came and went, the door banged loudly, so we didn't really get any peace and quiet on the second night. The grills and camp kitchen are well equipped, and the pools are nice too. Cinema for kids is every evening.
Großartiger Campingplatz mit allem, was man braucht. Bislang der beste an der Westküste und eine Erholungsoase nach einigen weniger guten. Top auch der Coffeetruck am Morgen.
Level large sites. Good amenities.
A busy park with exceptional amenities, pools and common areas.

Was a good holiday until the park mad us move our car offsite because we had a motor home and not a caravan so was treated differently to everyone else even though we used less of our tiny site than those with f250 and Isuzu trucks with caravans all on osame site won't be back here in a hurry probably take my 25 years with the RAC elsewhere also.

We stayed at a powered site in a  hire campervan. It was a great experience staying here. Super clean toilets and showers. The camp kitchen was unlike anything I’ve ever seen before - amazing!! Also a great location… very close to the Cadillac pub and a short walk into the little town. Would definitely recommend and we will be coming back to stay another time!
No hot water in ladies showers for 3days
This is a great caravan park well located excellent facilities.

I made a last minute booking here and staff were beyond helpful. Shuffled things around to make room and accommodate my requirements to fit with your bookings I had. Lovely experience. Customer service was a breathe of fresh air.
Excellent service and place for RV. Highly recommend.

We really didn't gel with this caravan park. First - the 2pm check in (latest we've personally encountered anywhere so far) is too late. Driving for hours to get here, you want to be able to check in to setup your van asap, not wait around in the carpark. It's a wasted day.

Secondly - the whole park just has a really negative vibe. Speaking to a couple of other neighbours staying there also agreed, it's hard to put your finger on what it may be exactly, but collectively think it's the people (most were very unfriendly, to the point of making you feel uncomfortable). Another older gent we spoke to at the Ningaloo caravan park described it "clicky".

The sites also back on to one another in a way that your awning sits in line with their awning out the back of you so you essentially sit together, which in our case was a negative experience due to who was next door. Upon backing our van in, there was also a next door neighbour blasting music from a radio on their outside table directly to our van, then coming out and staring at us like we'd done something wrong. Luckily her husband came home and told her to turn it down shortly afterwards. I was so overjoyed when they said they were leaving tomorrow after staying 3 weeks it was difficult to contain myself

On paper, it seems to have it all. Pools good, bbq areas, decent amenities, dump point, neat and tidy, but the feel was that bad we've cancelled our next stay after Osprey Bay and rebooked in at the Ningaloo caravan park just across the road, it's much nicer there (in my opinion). Was dreading having to go back so I'm glad they were able to refund our next stay. Others do apparently really enjoy it though as other comments state, so I'm just naturally more observant and notice these things moreso due to numerous factors. But if you've got a boat on the roof of your 4wd, don't care about anything and wish people would  just stay the heck away from you you might love it.
Great park.  Staff are very friendly and helpful.  $58 per night for power and water with RAC discount, September 2025

Schöner Campground, aber ziemlich weit weg zum Zentrum. Kein Zugang oder Fussweg zum Meer. Die Reception nimmt es an einem Tag sehr genau und lässt Leute die neu ankommen erst pünktlich um 2 pm herein , während an anderen Tagen Caravans  schon um 11 am herein gefahren kamen. Es kommt wahrscheinlich darauf an wer an der Reception sitzt. Wir kamen mit anderen Personen um 1.30 pm und mussten warten bis es genau 2 pm war. Es hatte genügend leere Stellplätze.
Excellent facilities and showers!

We did not pre-book as we had not planned to travel north of Kalbarri.
We called, not expecting to be able to get a powered site, and the lovely lady spent quite some time juggling sites and managed to get us in for 10 nights.
We were pleasantly surprised by the low cost of the site, expecting Exmouth to be very expensive.
It is a lovely park that is well laid out. The reception staff were super helpful and cheerful.
(They even have quality impact drivers available at no cost to help drive awning/annexe/tent pegs into the rock hard ground 👍)

Excellent Value and Great Stay!

My family and I recently stayed at this caravan park and was thoroughly impressed. It offered excellent value for money—everything you could need without breaking your budget.
The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, check-in was easy and overall an enjoyable experience. You can tell they genuinely care about their guests.
The amenities were spotless and well-maintained, which made a big difference. Clean bathrooms, tidy grounds, and everything in working order made our stay very comfortable.

It’s also in a great location and close to good pubs, the aquarium and beach.

Highly recommend this place and will definitely be back next time I’m in the area!

We greatly appreciated the park admin selecting an awesome site directly opposite the dump point for our stay. You could not imagine our surprise! - the experience was enhanced by the prevailing (unfavourable) tropical  breeze and a conga line of vehicles parked directly in front of our site to deliver the goods.

Great facilities and walking distance to cafes.
The caravan sites were very generous.
You can book any adventures via reception.
The visitors centre across the road is worth a look.

Absolutely recommend for caravaners.
Wir waren hier zwei Nächte mit unserem Campervan und waren sehr zufrieden. Schöne angenehme Atmosphäre und toller Pool! 👍
Nice caravan park decent size sites. Very friendly

Love RAC Exmouth and Exmouth. Been here twice and will be back again. Lovely helpful staff. Amenities very clean. 2 dump points, 2 pools and 2 awesome camp kitchens. Very good for a park so large. Pity we couldn't stay longer as it was booked out.
Stayed here just before Xmas when it was off season… perfect time to come !

Lady at the info counter was the best!!!
Helped a lot and convinced us to do the best things!!! Thanks so much x
Heaps of room for your car and caravan
Very basic cabin, $170/night, without toilet or shower!

Amazing new facilities but lacking a decent children playground (and maybe a jumping pillow). I heard it’s in the works for the future though!
Was a bit noisy given we were next to the
main road but overall great value and a good experience.
Don’t get there before 11:30 even if your in a van we arrived at 11:15 wouldn’t let us in
We have already booked for next year
Good sized sites and the widest access roads I've ever seen. Amenities good.

Stayed 2 nights in a blue reef studio. The room itself was fairly basic but clean and equipped with the basics. Some of the toilet/shower blocks were older but there were also a few nice new ones.
Kids had a ball of a time with cart hire/ games room. We didn't get to use the swimming pool. There was a nightly outdoor movie at 7pm. The on-site coffee cart in the morning makes great coffee and toasties.
This place is so good everything about it was great,nice big sites,pet friendly good clean amenities very relaxing stay
Good spot, good amenities, just wish the lights didnt stay on over night

Very nice camp close to everything you need to go. The ladies at the check in are lovely and welcoming. Facility is well managed and very clean. Showers spacious,  people are super friendly ( I believe thats generally all Australians ,we love the country 😀). There is a nice pool area and kids gaming area. Kitchen is very spacious and has all you will need . It could of been better if there is tarp was placed on some red dirt sand area but we do understand this is the nature, this is experience so be advised.  Also be aware this is a big camp meaning it can get busy so the kitchen gets packed during dinner. Plan your bookings very very ahead. There is also a coffee van in yhe mornings where u can grab toast, coffee/tea, toast, muffins,milkshake  and more. There is also a tour agency up front where you can easily book yours tours as you go.
Huge caravan park with lots of facilities.  Not on bore water 🙂
The facilities are good
Large caravan park with large gravel sites. Amenities are modern & clean with plenty of hot water.

Very large park with generous sites. Great facilities and family friendly. The only downside … which was a significant one in my book, was  finding numerous facilities electronically locked. Don't get gastro here!!  Other guests told me that it was a regular occurrence but it only happened to me the once. I rang reception and after waiting on hold for 4+ minutes I was told the staff would be over to open the doors manually. I gave up waiting. 🥺
Great little drive just out of Exmouth. Magic scenery and an easy drive.
Everything you need good water pressure
Great stay fantasic amenities, close to town with 2 boutique breweries which we're most enjoyable.

Great accommodation and camping grounds and close by to the shops!
Ammenities were clean, however there was very limited hot water in the kitchen sinks which made washing up tricky.

A very big park. Up to 400 sites. 2 very inviting swimming pools. Clean & well stocked amenities which are checked regularly throughout the day and into the evening. The water connection was good and there was no need to use the filter. I did not use the laundry so I cannot comment. We were quite happy there.
Nice caravan Park,  very busy,  good facilities and nice pool
Great park

Super caravan park!
The facilities are like new and very clean.
The staff are lovely.
We had initially booked a single night, but our campervan broke down and the tow truck couldn't pick us up until 2 days later.
Despite our reservations being fully booked, the lady at reception found a solution so that we could spend an extra night on our pitch.
Thank you again

Great all round facilities
Very friendly,  helpful front office.

Great for all types of camping
If not great yourself, one of their self-contained cabins
Kid and animal friendly
Really enjoyed myself and my family our holiday their
I'm definitely going back
Great park, central to everything, lovely pools, showers (although, cold most of the time!), all facilities and amenities in great clean condition. Sites are ample with hard packed gravel.
Awesome stay. Friendly Staff and good amenities available. Good luck driving pegs into the ground without a drill.

Park is big and quite crowded but quite spacious sites and room to park van, car etc
As with most parks in WA more expensive than some great parks elsewhere in Aus. Locals agree and call it the tourism tax!
Nice pool, camp kitchen, easy check in
Close to town beach.
Downside is that park camping sites are mostly on red dirt so don’t expect things to stay clean and ablution blocks really need cleaning more than once a day as can be covered in red mud.
An on site cafe would add to its appeal

If you are thinking of staying here make sure you ask questions. We booked a room apparently it was the only one available at that time, no ensuite no parking at the room. At check-in I was showen a map of room location and a single parking spot, I asked about parking for my boat. The response was put your car or your boat in the parking space available and park the other on the street. When at the parking the sign said no boats and there was no way of backing a boat into a bay anyway so both car and boat on the street so much for secure parking.
The room was clean but noisy even with earplugs still hear everything, the power went out at 5:30am and a floodlight came on in the room to wake you up that was great.
Shower and toilet facilities well for the prices they are charging I would expect a little better. Tiles in shower floors broken and cracked usual old storey with shower head half working water would go hot and cold during shower, I was the only one there at the time.
Camp kitchen was good clean usable but had to pick your times as it got busy.
Staff seemed friendly and tried to help with my parking problems but no management on site to speak with.

What a disappointment. If you love camping in the middle of the Rockingham suburbs then you’ll love this place. Caravans all squished in like sardines and hardly any trees. RAC you have confused a caravan park with a carpark. A patch of grass is for those privileged few. If you are in a tent forget about it. The ground is so hard you have to borrow a drill from reception, but don't worry it wont make any difference. If you want privacy you will need to pay a premium. Keep everything under lock and key. Ive never felt so disappointed in a caravan park. This place simply exists to accomodate the masses….skip it and stay elsewhere.
I had no privacy

Pretty good. You can hear everyone entering their codes to get into the toilets at night. We were also relaxing one day and they had the toilets and caravan dump station pumped out, it was discusting and made us feel sick.
We had a good time and a great facility. The toilets and kitchen are well-cleaned.

Prepare to have you stuff covered in dust all the time, if you have to peg anything down either bring a drill or borrow the ones from the office but dont worry the pegs will bend anyway and they wont be easy to get out, you may as well shovel dust into everything as you pack up because thats going to happen anyway. Dust, hard dusty ground and no shade.
Power and water hookup. But most site were very dusty. Close to shops, pubs and restaurants.

Receptionist who booked us in was abrupt, uninterested and unfriendly. Not welcoming or volunteering information. Seemed every question we had was annoying. Rushed through site location. Attitude was rude. Sent us to visitor
centre Cafe in 37°heat  closed and always closes early on a sat!  Woman in adjoining shop very  friendly and helpful as were staff in visitor centre.
Such a beautifully clean, modern and well set out park! Great value for money, friendly staff who go above and beyond to ensure you have a comfortable stay. Clean facilities. Just perfect :)

Was sandwiched between two large caravans and a family who set up the tent-mahal at 9:30pm with a drill. They kept shining their lights up into my tent. The park was mostly empty in March, yet everyone was in one corner of the park. Mainly booked the place for the showers after a long hot drive, but there was no reprieve as the water didn’t get colder than the 40 degree air outside.

Great caravan park with great facilities.
Good sized powered sites
Camp kitchen is well sized with lots of room for everyone
for anyone unaware make sure you bring screw in tent pegs and a drill otherwise you will have to buy them from the caravan store which isn't open on weekends. Otherwise a great experience
Fantastic.  Easy access to everything we needed and the staff are friendly.  Come for a visit.
Large van spaces, clean and new amenities, nice staff, really I have nothing bad to say about this place.

RAC was an awesome park! The facilities are top-notch and very modern, with multiple pools to enjoy — perfect for a relaxing getaway. The check-in and check-out process was so convenient, all done via text message, which made everything super easy. It’s a great park that really prioritizes guest comfort and convenience. I highly recommend it!

We stayed in a family cabin and had a great experience. The staff were accommodating of a late check in and also gave great reccomendations for activities. The room had been renovated to be more modern, bathrooms were clean and had great shower pressure. Good location 20min walk into town

Stayed 13 nights at this lovely caravan park, very friendly and welcoming staff
Ablution blocks were always very clean and to a very high standard
Highly recommend a stay here

Site is well located however unfortunately we haven’t found the receptionist very friendly or helpful. The site lacks shade in comparison to its neighbour Ningaloo and communal spaces such as kitchens, toilets and showers could be better maintained!
Ich war mit Camper unterwegs

'No unpowered sites available' was said at reception. I then booked a more expensive powered site, before I saw that nearly every unpowered site was unused. This is a very unsympathetic way to make more money ;)
Pools are supposed not to be used "after dark", fun thing there are closed already at 6.
Kitchen and showers are good though. Still never gonna come here again, since I hate being lied to.
Camp kitchen great, staff awesome and friendly, location fantastic. Two pools to choose from, nice and refreshing ☺️
We had a nice experience at RAC Exmouth. Budget friendly cabin with air-conditioning, TV, fridge and the share amenities where in good nick. It is a must to stay at a place with AC during summer.
Nice complex, amenities are good.
Nice place the area was big well maintained ， but the cabin we stayed got some smell from last customer

I stayed for a few days at the campsite and definitely had a wonderful experience with my small group of friends.

First, I want to highlight that the reception staff were incredibly polite and welcoming. The RAC unit has an excellent location, close to the main avenue and shops, which made everything very convenient.

I visited during summer, and it was extremely hot. The campsite has two pools, which was great. However, I believe some improvements could be made. The larger pool is spacious, but adding a shade cover over most of it would make a huge difference, as there aren’t many shaded areas, and the water gets very warm under the sun. Because of this, I ended up using the smaller pool, which had refreshingly cold water. However, since everyone was using it, the water was not as clean as I would have expected.

In terms of cleanliness, I felt it could have been better. While the area is generally clean, every time I visited the bathrooms, they didn’t seem to be very well maintained.

The game room is well-equipped, but it requires additional payment to use. I think it would be better if it was included in the camping fee, as it could be a great entertainment option for all guests without extra costs.

Waste management could also be improved. The bins were often full whenever I tried to dispose of my trash, so more frequent collection would definitely help.

Lastly, I was surprised that the kitchen had specific opening and closing hours. I had never stayed at a campsite where the kitchen wasn’t available 24/7. This limitation was a bit inconvenient at times.

Overall, it was a good experience, but with a few improvements, it could be exceptional.
Have to be up their with the best caravan parks

We had a powered site.  The amenities were faultless the kitchens were outstanding, the bathrooms were sparkling.
We were there Xmas day, so many wall ovens, hot plates, sinks and barbecues. Huge camping fridges, definitely better than anything else we have seen.  Thanks so much to the management and staff, we appreciated this so much.
The camping site is well equipped with everything you need enclosed by all the amenities you will need
nice location, nice amneties, everything is pretty cool

Fairly mordent amenities in the center of Exmouth. Really clean shower and toilet building. Not too much dust around. The plot are not so big but there is some for bigger rig on the other side. Kitchen with all needed gears.
Excellent service and staff
Loved this campsite and the showers were amazing ! Lovely pool to! Definitely worth a visit !
Great caravan park but a little expensive, very little shade on power sites. Friendly staff.
Site are plenty room and the amenities are clean

First time travelling through WA and I’m very impressed with this camp ground. It’s very well maintained, kept clean and tidy. Great BBQ and kitchen facilities. Friendly reception staff and great value for money.
Well equipped, clean, and extremely comfortable.

The place is great but they charged me one night more, their explanation was due to a misunderstanding (from their side) and they wouldn't accept refunds
I mean, is this acceptable

We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin. It was as shown on the web site and was clean and fully functional and had everything we needed.
The park is very well maintained, clean and has a few pools.
We found the staff to be very well organised, helpfull and friendly.
This is the second time I have stayed there and will do again I am sure.

This is a popular place to stay, even in the off season it was busy. Amenities are clean and the shower heads are brilliant. It has two pools that shut at sunset, great reception area with a shop you can book tours from. If you get there before midday you can book a 4pm sunset glass bottom boat. A bus will pick you up and drive you to the marina. Looked like one of the best caravan places to stay.
Nice place. Poor service but lovely grounds. Camp kitchen is lovely to use. Coffee van us handy but ridiculously expensive for tiny cups of coffee.
Lovley pools and shower blocks.  Best equipped camp kitchen we found on oir 13k road trip round Australia. Piches were quite close together. Had a lovely week at this campite.

My partner and I couldn’t be happier with our stay. We have been camping by we scored a campsite in the shade literally right into the if the showers and toilets! The staff are very friendly and the campsite is very peaceful with all the facilities that are needed. The location is fabulous. Recommend to anyone!

A big park with plenty of powered and drinkable water sites. Amenities are clean and they have 2 fantastic camp kitchens. Great location close to the town centre and great base to do the ningaloo Coast drive.
Great location, clean tidy rooms. Only reason for 4* and not 5 is showers aren’t the cleanest.

We stayed here for 4 nights, what a great caravan park. Camp kitchen is one of the best I have seen. We had an ensuite site very nice. Staff are very friendly I have nothing but nice things to say about this park.

Easy to book on online or over the phone call. The receptionist was lovely and the housekeepers too. Always clean at the kitchen, swimming pool and the bathroom. We have stayed there twice now at powered site camp and we will definitely come back again.

Kill joy staff ‘you can’t take 2 cars to the cabin’, only 1 car per cabin… excuse me? Boats are registered vehicles and cabins had both car and boat… caravans are registered vehicles so that means 2 vehicles per site!! Not carrying all my stuff from the street to the cabin, loads of space, just rude to have that rule. (Desk staff were polite, following the rules which are rubbish rules)
Cabin was great, clean and comfortable.
Love to go there again in the future

Great locations, some of the staff were great, one lady refused to help/accomodate our needs said there was no room to move closer to the rest of our group, went to another lady and she looked and found a spot without any issues

Super Campingplatz. Sauber, zwei Pools, sehr ruhig obwohl sehr viele Familien da sind. Am Morgen gibt es einen Caffeewagen mit sehr gutem Kaffe und kleinen Snacks. Ein Informationsbüreo wo man Touren buchen kann direkt neben der Rezeption. Ein Strand wo man mit dem Auto nach unten fahren kann und seine Tische und Stühle raustellen kann direkt unterhalb (3min fahren, 20 Minuten laufen) Würden wir definitiv wieder kommen.

Wonderful park! There are multiple amenities and laundry buildings so you are never in a cue for either. All facilities are spotless clean. Staff were lovely and accomodating. Two pools are awesome. We recommend 🙌🏼
Nasty customer services by Kelly!!!

Lovely big sites, apparently we were in the newer area and the showers and toilets are spotless! Fantastic camp kitchen and games room. Loved having two pools to chose from.
Very friendly helpful staff, when we were going to have to move sites they juggled things around so that we didn't have to in the end that was a huge help!!
Love that the park is dog friendly and within walking distance of a dog friendly beach, it doesn't look much especially at low tide but the dogs loved it for cooling off.
Good location for everything.

Nice Caravan Park with large sites. Excellent amenities for everyone to enjoy including a games room, two swimming pools, souvenirs, and a matching pair kitchen areas. I arrived at the caravan park at 11.01 to check in but was told that I was too early and was directed to leave the park and would that I would be
notified by sms when the site was ready. I left the park as directed and then received a sms approx 15 minutes later to inform me that I can now come back and set up on my allocated site. There is no grass on the sites so I am puzzled as to what the check in problem and delay was about.

Would expect more from this place. Give me a call if you would like me to show you how to clean the amenities properly. There are products that will clean the floors spotless but obviously you people aren't aware of them. Bit disappointed

Comfortable rooms, we asked to park our boat in front of our room instead of parking our car which was approved but later that day received an aggressive text that we were violating the rules so we then needed to explain we had permission and we weren’t the only people who had their boats and cars parked in the parking area when there was actually plenty of space
A great location, excellent communication and easy to book. The Studio accommodation suits us perfectly.
Hard to have a sleep in with people moving around as early as 4 and 5 am

Camera decorosa, niente di speciale. Nota positiva la piscina ben tenuta e molto curata.
Per il resto è un campeggio assolato nè più nè meno

We recently stayed a few nights at the RAC Holiday Park on the main road in the centre of Exmouth. This is a large facility with hundreds of dedicated spots for a variety of campervan, motorhome, tent spots and motel style units. All is available on their website where you can reserve your stay.

They have an internal entry for parking, large lobby and check-in area, tour operator services and some basic food & drinks available. There is a nice pool, communal kitchen setup and coffee truck open during the mornings.

During our stay we booked one of their motel units and found it quite sufficient for those traveling around W.A. with rental cars. Space was pretty good, simple but fit for purpose, especially if you are really only using the room as a place to sleep in the evenings. There is table, basic kitchenette, for heating up and eating simple & prepared meals not cooking. Bathroom did the job just fine, TV, A/C and lightening all worked just fine. Bed was comfortable as well. Wifi didn't work particularly well during our stay, was quite slow during our stay.

Overall, we liked the communal feel and overall setup of the RAC in Exmouth. Location is good as well. There are restaurants and Ningaloo Visitor and Discovery Centre within short walking distance and you are only 30-45 minutes drive to the Cape Range NP hiking areas and Ningaloo coastline beaches. Would gladly stay here again next time in Exmouth.

It's a great park but needs a heap of maintenance and cleaning. The amount of money this place pulls they need to keep up.
Also need to implement scan cards for facilities to reduce non paying personal using them.
Beautiful park in close proximity to everything. Awesome camp kitchen. Julie in the shop/tourist bookings is very helpful and knowledgeable. Toilets are spotless and highly recommend this park

We stayed 3 nights (would have loved more) and loved every part of the park, from Reception, Booking Office, Coffee Van, excellent clean amenities, very generous powered sites, amazing camp kitchen, games room, beautiful resort style swimming pool, we could not have ask for more. We would recommend this place to anyone and everyone.

Camp kitchen was kept very clean unlike our own accommodation and ablution blocks. The floor in our unit was full of sand when we arrived, the ablution blocks were not cleaned in the three days we were there. RAC, you need to do a lot better.

We have been coming to this Caravan Park for 3 years now. The sites are large enough.  It’s a lovely park staff are so welcoming and friendly/ helpful,  the check in is easy. The park has two great pools and great camp kitchen and gamesroom. We love the location walk to town and the pubs and restaurants and beach at the back. All the amenities are very new and fresh / cleaned every day.
Unfortunately the amenities on emu way are disgustingly old and well and truly badly need upgraded in line with the rest of the other amenities . I cannot understand why the RAC do not upgrade them they are horrible. I’ve seen many people having  to walk to the other showers and toilets it’s a shame. But over all a great place to come to.
We stayed in 2 different cabins, one with ensuite bathroom/ toilet. Both were good, but prefer the ensuite unit. Camp kitchen and BBQ area excellent, well appointed and kept very clean. Nice vibe

This is a great little spot in town if you are looking to park up for a few days. The newer pool area with adjoining kitchen, play and bathroom amenities is the nicest little corner of the whole place. We also really enjoyed the coffee/brekky cart that opened each morning near the reception. If you need a spot in town, come here!
Very popular park.  Situated right on the beach.  Staff were professional and friendly.

We stayed here in June on powered site. It is a huge park and very dusty. So cars go around fast spreading dust. Our site had some grass. I was disappointed that next to us was 2 cars boat and two tents on site. Which was unpleasant that we were a couple and next site was large group.

Nice park,good size caravan sites,place was over run with young,unsupervised children riding scooters and bikes in front of moving cars,shower and toilet blocks need updating or at least the soap holders and hooks put back in showers that have been broken off,do not accept site near camp kitchen and shower block,very noisy,people walking through site all the time,no seperation  between clothes line and caravan,should be better for amount of $ paid

Would have got 5 stars if for the amount you paid per night was spent on a decent mattress and not the cheapest garbage you get from the Harvey Norman seconds store.
Factor in a trip to your physio when you get home.
It's probably the worst bed I've slept in.

Disappointed in RAC. The staff are polite and friendly, however it’s the company that has let us down. I called to request a site and put 2 cars on it, I got told one car per site. So I say okay I guess we will cop it since it’s the only place with availability in Exmouth. Then they charge us $240 for two nights (EXPENSIVE). We get here and the sites are HUGE and can EASILY fit 2 cars. Ripped off and scammed. Plus the showers don’t heat up and you have to run them for like 5 minutes until they do which is a waste of water. Just another case of a big chain company doing what they do best and ripping off the small people.

Nice site plenty of amenities which was also good  road is a bit narrow trying to reverse  in site nice and wide tho why people park close to road in front of their van makes it a bit more awkward  great kitchen tho and barbies sometimes a bit dangerous  with the kiddies flying around have to be very careful  nice friendly  reception

Great park in a great location. Very spacious caravan sites. Well kept pools. Fantastic for younger kids.
Dog friendly Park.
Toilets and showers need a lot more attention, obviously the site manager and supervisor are not doing there jobs properly by doing inspections of these important area's. Photos attached are seconds after the cleaners had finished 😳😖

Pretty over-priced,
Minimal Amenities,
That's Exmouth for you
Dated compared to refurbished sites. Great location, with activities for the kids.

We were placed close to the camp kitchen and games room, both of which are clean and very well resourced. They are unfortunately very noisy and air hockey can be heard as late as 10 and as early as 6.30. The pools are immaculate.  We found the staff to be very helpful and polite. The site is very popular with young families so expect them to be up bright and early and their parents to not keep them quiet till a reasonable time.
Always enjoy staying here. Just easy and casual. Friendly staff. Close to town facilities.
Awesome place to stay at Beautiful pools great service,big sites.Clean amenities very friendly staff.

Very good park overall. Amenity blocks need more attention to cleaning. If you want a quiet stay keep away from the pools and kitchen areas. A lot of noise comes from both. The caravan sites are good sizes.
big sites. But the roads are so narrow. Very hard to reverse into bays. You can see they have put as many sites as they can into the area to the detriment of road space

One of the most modern, spacious, and well resourced caravan parks I’ve seen!
We had 2 swags in a powered site. Toilets and kitchen looked very new with plenty of them scattered throughout the park.

Great bbqs and easy check in process
Minimal grass sites but excellent location

I did the whale shark tour, booked 3 months in advance. Highly recommend
Ningaloo discovery is the tour I went with

Very nice park.  Booked in December 2023 and got a site that doesnt get booked except for busy times.  Pwople around us booked only 3 m9ntgs ago explain that.  Just be aware that you do not want to be put on site 312.  This site is right next to the dump point and I mean right next to it.  So everytime someone empties their toilet you will hear the bang of the lid opening and an even bigger bang as it gets slammed shut.  Sadly also lucky it was on the last night it started to smell really bad.   They did offer for us to move but we had already completely set up.  Probably the wrong decision.
Facilities are due an upgrade but room are nice!
Good location, shops just up the road and a pub across the road. Staff friendly and helpful. A few local breweries serving good food and quality beer within walking distance.
Old and dirty facilities. But otherwise nice and quiet park. Pool was really nice, location ist great.
Une soirée et une nuit en campervan. Lieu agréable, belles commodités. Je recommande

Amazing staff who provided assistance when our car broke down and we couldn't move our van
Very clean amenities and all staff friendly and helpful

Great place to stay and has large sites. Campers kitchens are excellent.
It's a large area and usually quite full. Two excellent pools and very close to the shopping centre and IGA.
2 excellent caravan and camping stores within a km from each other.
Exmouth is a surprising place. Very quirky and country style. Laid back and usually excellent service.

This was hands down a perfect place to stay.
We stated in a cabin by the pool.
Place spotless,grounds beautiful.
The place is great it’s clean central and has great facilities

Room was not what we booked online
Disappointing experience,
No refund expected
Would definitely not recommend
If anyone would like to discuss further happy to assist

Great caravan park, sites are clean and spacious. Swimming pools are beautiful and well looked after. We got the ensuite bathroom, it was modern and clean - n absolute treat.

Coffee van on site in the morning with very friendly service, decent playground and games room (bring your own table tennis paddle and ball though).

Park is close to Town Beach and walking distance to Whalebone and a few other key locations.

Will definitely be back!
Was there for a couple of days, went to turquoise beach, nice weather

Really bad staff, never met so rude people. I felt really unwelcoming. Weird things are going on, someone died in the pool in march. And they didn’t tell the people staying there. Would really not recommend you stay here. We changed to Ningaloo caravan park on the other side it’s quite nice. They have aircon in the kitchen and the pool is full of living people.
We fell so much more welcome here, and can finally enjoy exi 🙌🏽

It is a huge park on the main road, with various accommodation options.

We went powered site - wide, flat, hard sand with some shade. Downside was not drivethrough & the streets are very narrow & with other guests having their 4wds parked in front of their van reversing was a challenge (we're in a 8mtr motorhome).

Checkin staff were friendly with info texted prior to arrival. Checkin quick. Office has some tourist info, basic shop & a large tour shop with souvenirs & apparel etc.

Amenities are good with multiple blocks - many with laundries.  2 pools are popular as are games rooms. Multiple camp kitchens.

Security on vehicle entry & exits. Right next door to visitor centre. Walk to various restaurants,  cafes, bars & groceries.

Avoid if booking unpowered emplacement. They are all badly situated, and you will end up on the main driveway of the camping with big rig towing caravan and boat starting 4 am to 10 pm.

Spot had litter and forgotten dug pegs, which we were lucky not to drive unto.

Old kitchen is as it sounds, old, with fridge and light bulbs not working.

Staf were helpful and responsive, but it wasn't enough to compensate for the lack of good camp site design and general care and maintenance.

Overall, it was underwhelming for a RAC.
Great place with easy access. Great pool areas. Camp kitchen is brilliant.

Great location in the centre of Exmouth.
Sites and amenities showing wear and tear, customer service wasn't always good and cleanliness of the park wasn't the best.
Great place to stay. Good facilities, great studio room. Very good air conditioning. Close to some brilliant food and shops.

We had a motor home for three night over Easter holidays, the park was full (of course). 5 stars for ‘room’ because site was well maintained, spacious, tree shade, flat and clear access to water/power. Great clean amenities, everything you need in kitchen, big space, bbqs, two pools (it’d be awesome if one was kid free over the holidays 😅) $5 washington machine, unlimited wifi, super friendly service and great coffees from the coffee cart. We walked to the whalebone brewery a couple of time and can walk the road to see sunset/sunrise over the water. Can walk to info centre too 👌 expensive but fair given when we were there. Would go back

The checkin process was really quick and easy.  Pools were constantly FULL FULL FULL of people (mostly little people), ladies toilet block door never shut properly (for safety reasons I suggest they get this fixed) and both the mens and ladies toilets were disgustingly dirty.  I get they need a refurbishment but they could at least give it a rub over with scrubbing brush once a week. Taps leaked in the amenities but they suggest you shower for only 4 minutes. The other thing that needed a clean was the dump point - it too needs a scrubbing brush. Our powered caravan site was all dirt, however there are a very small number of grass sites available.  Did see the police do a drive through which ads to the security.
Went there around end of February and it was really quiet. We stayed in a Blue Reef Queen room. The camp kitchen and the toilets and showers were clean. The two pool areas are amazing.

A late, very late review. I have just seen that I didn't review this during our stay some years ago.

A great, large camp site right in town. We would have liked more shade.
Great caravan park. Very clean kitchen.

This place is huge and well organise. It wasn’t super busy when we stayed in mid March and you could enjoy everything without any waiting time. There are two swimming pools that you can choose. The facilities such as the camp kitchen and bathrooms are new, big and always clean. The camp sites, powered and not, are unsealed but the management are making also some of the site with grass. During our stay, we found two emu walking around the sites everyday looking for food - please do not feed them. The park is also dog friendly, probably the only one in Exmouth. We had a really good experience at Rac holiday Park but I would really appreciate if one the two swimming pool or even both, the dogs were allowed just in the grass area so while you are having a break from the hot weather, they can also enjoy a bit of grass and shadow.
Camped there, dry dusty car park not trees or shade very open

Had a very bad service experience with this RAC. Cannot recommend to book early as they do not let you change your booking less than 72 hours in advance (in our case we were not able to make the drive up in time on our roadtrip) - so they take away any flexibility, while at the same time not even being half full this time of year (beginning of March). When we got there shortly after 5 pm the reception was already closed (the earliest we have experienced on this WA roadtrip). While they did leave an envelope with access key to open the barrier in the entrance, this code did not work after many tries. We were lucky enough that another guest gave us their code to even get in. We also left a day early since it was too hot for us (would recommend rather staying on one of the wilder campsites on the other coast such as osprey bay as there is a breeze and more comfortable temperatures) - however a day later they charged us another night on top as they say we have not checked out on time (despite the fact that we had been gone for a night already, never used the access code so they had no proof we were ever even there, and obviously nobody checked the campsite to see we were not there) - the common practice on all other campgrounds we stayed at in WA was that we paid for the days we planned to be there and then either extended actively or just left - none of the others charged an additional night when we had left in time before checkout.
So far they did not even come back to our complain regarding the extra charged night.
At this park you will find the staff really helpful. As well as the ground keepers..can highly recommended  that you say there.
Bad service. The staff was unpleasant and mean. Flee this place!

I would like to give a shout out to the cleaners, they were always friendly and smiley and the amenities were always very clean and organized! Camp sites were big, but not many trees for shade or shelter from windy days.

Très déçu du service client, j’avais besoin d’une visseuse pour 30 minutes afin de réparer mon lit dans ma voiture, la personne de l’accueil et une personne de la maintenance nous a remballer sans nous écouter ou comprendre notre besoin, surtout qu’ils ont tous les équipements sur place, qu’on me prête pas l’outil je veux bien comprendre mais que les personnes ne soit pas sympa et pas à l’écoute du client ce n’est pas cool.
Got some funky emu’s, some funkier guests
Nice campground with large sites. Facilities were clean and the wifi at our site was great. Cost us $51 for one night.

Imagine, if you will, a sparsely planted carpark. That's it.
Nice facilities overall.
It must be said that if you like your Australian birds IN YOUR FACE this is the perfect place. I felt like snow white. But with huge emus IN YOUR FACE. As a bird person I was delighted, ornithophobes may well die however.
We loved it, nice clean cabin's and great staff

Nice park. Good facilities but at $56 per night pretty expensive compared to other parks in area. On a pension makes it unaffordable for more than a couple of nights even with members  discount. Good location to town. Stayed in caravan site so can't comment on rooms.

Well maintained and immaculate accommodation. Team is impeccably professional and helpful and provided excellent tour suggestions and guides of which we have benefited extremely from. Cosy atmosphere with views of starry night right outside the room. Will always recommend.

The caravan park does not accommodate for campers and closes half the park without letting campers know. Booked 2 months in advance and put on a dusty overflow parking area with no shade and hard gravel ground and advised how lucky we were to even get a camp-site. They will cram you in like a sardine in a tin and charge you the earth for it.
Toller Campingplatz,  sehr heiss hier, unglaublich sauber...Die Campingküche ein Traum,  wie in Großräschen, tolle Aussengrillplätze...Pool sehr cool...braucht man bei den hohen Temperaturen
I love it the pools and the hospitality it is a definite recommendation
Thank you Alana for helping to make our stay really great! Friendly staff and facilities are super clean. We’ll be back for sure.

Our last stay here was in 2018 and we believe the park has gone downhill since then. Our site was a good size and easily accessed. Most sites however are very hard gravel and quite dusty. Yes, I know we're in northern Australia but some parks can still manage to slab or mat sites to keep the dust and dirt levels down. I would definitely recommend booking a slab or mat site here as there are some available. The amenities are tired and in need of an upgrade. Some toilets and showers out of order. Both camp kitchens areas are modern and pretty well appointed. There are two pool areas.
All in all it was a bit disappointing for us. If motoring organisations are going to invest in these parks then we expect a better product.

What a Joke!!!!!!
It’s 40’ everyday and the pool is closed twice in a week . It’s 6pm and the pool is closed but it’s still 35’
This place is living on cloud 9 as we will never be back .
Disgusted
Well it’s very underwhelming to be honest . Camp site with Ens is average at best .Park is generally dirty with no real care for guests . Unless you have a van the hard stand on the campground is useless .
Not really interested in the swimming pool with so many Young kids and families . Lots of older people that needs servicing as well .
I didn’t think coins for a washing machine was still a thing in 2023 but obviously Exmouth didn’t get the memo .
One good thing is the Ens is very clean and tidy . Never again though

Arrived after hours and found our site double booked. After hours help number was unknown to me and was mentioned only upon mentioning the issue at reception the next day.
No compensation whatsoever offered when booking a second night. Poor customer service in my experience.
Pool kinda dirty when i was there. Pool is salt water.
Value for money was heaps better in Monkey Mia.

This is basically a giant parking lot. There is no privacy, no shade, no trees, sites are all next to each other, all just gravel ground. Facilities are top and clean and fantastic, probably very suitable for families with caravans, but besides that it is not a pleasant camping experience. Sites could use a lot more love.

Highly recommend this Park. We have actually based ourselves here for the duration of our Ningaloo Reef exploring - perfect. Right across the road from the Information Centre. Fabulous large site, excellent amenities,  friendly helpful service. Loved the added bonus of emu visits 😀

This was the most expensive campground we have booked in Western Australia. From the infrastructure it would also be the most modern with beautiful and big showers. However, it was unfortunately the most disgusting campsite we have experienced. EVERY morning EACH toilet had poo traces (ladies as well as men) and towels were left lying around. We don’t know if it probably was because of the many unattended kids?
The campsite is completely designed for families with children, therefore it was not really a relaxed atmosphere as in other campsites.
In addition, the wifi unfortunately did not really work and the trash containers were always overflowing.
At this price we expect something else.
A plus where the big sized sites.
Very good place to stay.

Nice place to spend the night before spending a few nights in the national park. Laundry facilities were very clean, showers also. A very comfortable last night in a powered site before going off grid in our camper van :-)

Staff on reception are lovely and super helpful. My room (Reef Queen) was comfortable, well appointed and clean, though an in-room kettle would be a useful addition. The other facilities are where this resort fell down - camp kitchen was clean but had no supplies to speak of, two of the fridges were out of order, no sink plugs or hot water for washing up. The bathrooms were pretty dingy and need an upgrade. I stayed 3 nights and didn't have a single (short) shower that I got hot water consistently regardless of time of day or other usage. The thing that I was most shocked at was the criminal water wastage at this property - located in a desert, using bore water, signs everywhere urging users to save water but no low flow shower heads, no low flow taps, no sink plugs, limited hot water (so you have a longer shower waiting for it to come back on), no time-outs on taps or showers and the lawn sprinklers are constantly on watering dirt, concrete and a bit of grass. Even in the middle of the day. RAC you have a long way to go on your eco credentials. Please deal with this!!!

Excellent pools, kitchen and amenities. Compressed gravel campsites require screw in pegs. It would be good if RAC 'deposit - loaned' appropriate screw in pegs for use. Hammering in pegs is not possible - if they are longer than a (supplied) drill bit they protrude dangerously. Also if pegs are smaller than drilled holes they won't stay in!  Compressed gravel right to edge of trees will also stress trees and that is why they won't grow to give proper shade - compressed road base should be removed around trees in a 1m or so circle to provide for water etc. Better shading would also cool the area down.
Clean and well managed caravan park.

We had a very pleasant stay in Exmouth.  A few things have changed since my last visit,  but still enjoyable.  The park was mildly busy and had a feeling of it winding down before the school holiday crush.  It's a nice environment to stay.

Had to cancel my accomodation, which was not refundable - no problems - I knew this. My disappointment lies in the fact that this accomodation (cancelled more than 30 days in advance) is able to now be booked by someone else, and if this happened we remain out of pocket. "The forfeiture applies regardless of if the site is resold" not anywhere in the cancellation policy, but quoted by an employee. I do not wish the site to lose their income and if the site remains empty I am happy to pay, however if it is booked by someone else it would be nice to get my money refunded. Thank you, but you wont be "welcoming us in the future".

Nice, clean facilities. Will be better once the trees mature so there is more shade. Bays are large which is a plus, the ground in the newest part of the park is very hard. Dogs allowed but it would be great if they could add a fenced area for the dogs to play off lead, then I would say it was dog friendly.

Suprised I was not offered a credit as we broken down awaiting a mechanic at Bullara station I advised RAC exmouth we cannot make tomorrows stay explaining i broke down in the drive here (I needed 2 jumpstarts to make it to our site.)

Can only assume they are double dipping as it was said they have cancelled my booking just no redund
Clean and tidy
In a chalet.   Red dust everywhere.  A transportable.  Other accommodation the same cost in Exmouth but better quality. Advise people to compare accommodation...
Great central location, everything u need to enjoy a break. Friendly staff, good amenities
Clean and convenient
Very efficient park, well run and helpful staff. Small shop for booking tours etc, mobile coffee van and 2 swimming pools. Walk into town.  Expensive town all round but that’s part of remote living
Sites big.  Park is huge.  Plenty of toilet blocks.  Pools nice. Close to most shops and eating.
Well set out park.  Good sites, someone has to be near the dump point.  Good roads, plenty of room for getting the Big Rig around.

WIFI service unusable.
Non Telstra phone connectivity very poor.
TV reception marginal, CH10 only reasonably good picture. Most other channels hit and miss.
Nice pool area, good BBQs, caravan sites big enough, you do need to use the reception provided masonry drill to get pegs into the ground.
Very good ablution blocks.

OK distance from main town strip driving. Would be a long walk.

Pay the extra if you can to have your own shower in room. We didn't realise for 140 a night we wouldn't have a shower. Bit annoying having to go all the way to toilets in night.
Water in kitchen tastes a bit rough.

Overall ok but we would choose somewhere else next time.
Best place in Exmouth, we stayed twice. Book well in advance! Thanks Sarah on the front desk for such a cheerful welcome, you made our day!
Amazing place, great staff, room was clean and family friendly.
Good level powered and water sites on crushed hard gravel. Little shade but ablutions clean although fairly old. Large park and generally well presented - recommended

We enjoyed our stay at this park. But you will need to book well ahead to secure a site here, we had a Powered caravan site 7x nights. Was busy In July we had a fairly roomy site, all very hard gravel so pegs for awning difficult if you only have a hammer ! Amenities were kept very clean given the amount of people. Great camp kitchen & pool area. Tours picked up from front of park & handy having information centre & aquarium across the road. Good burgers from tavern across the road too. Highly recommend.
Just a day visitor. Enjoyed the coffee van
Good location. Some of the camping alleys are not paved making the site a bit dusty. Playground was closed.

What a let down. $75 a night to park in a dust bowl next to the main road. Can't check you in until after 12:30. What for? Definitely don't need to mow the site made entirely of crusher dust. Pin codes on amenities are a pain, too low and poorly lit to see them at night. To top it of the kids playground is not in use at the moment. Possibly the worst value van park in our last 5 months on the road. Glad we leave for North Lefroy tomorrow.

The park was nice, owners seem to genuinely care about guests. Facilities were lovely, modern, clean, plentiful and well maintained. The location however was a disaster. Right on the main road through town which is busy day and night and very loud. The sites are quite close together so even though we were only in a swag we felt squashed in to others. By far the worst thing is the pub across the road, so loud until late, went till 2am on the Saturday. We were there 3 nights and left exhausted.
If it was in a quieter location I’d definitely recommend it, but where it is now I wouldn’t go back and wouldn’t recommend it to friends or family, such a shame.
Stayed here for a couple of weeks. Very popular caravan park.

Super busy campground but was very lonely and dark going to the toilet in the night. Toilets are situated away from the campers behind the kitchen and games room which makes it quiet and lonely and isn’t well lite with no lock on entry door to lady’s bathrooms. Didn’t feel safe going there at night.

Sara at reception was amazing. Lovely super friend women who told us amazing snorkelling spots. She made our holiday so thank you Sara!!
Large caravan park with everything required. Toilets and showers are new and clean. The sandy, dusty ground is rock hard though.

We are very happy with our stay at RAC Exmouth.
The kitchen was large with multiple cook tops and BBQS, very clean. Plenty of indoor and out door seating.
Showers are amazing, better than my shower at home!
I'll be looking out for rac parks along the rest of our trip as they will now be our preferred park to stay with.

Campsites were a good size... amenities were neat ,clean .
Camp kitchen was amazing...2 swimming pools, very nicely set out . All in all a very family friendly park... Helpful n friendly staff..

Very dusty caravan park, Rac should put in concrete bays, amenities clean and showers good, over priced, $92 for an ensuite caravanbay???? A bit expensive,  enjoyed our stay but wouldn't bother returning.
Just arrived and have been very impressed with the facilities especially the camp kitchen just the best I have seen. It has 4 full ovens to cook with 😊😊😱
Awesome place kid friendly central to town good spot

So glad we chose to stay at RAC Exmouth. Staff were all friendly and welcoming. Accommodating multiple requests to extend our stay was never a problem. Sites were large, amenities were spotlessly clean and spacious. We also loved relaxing by the resort style pool and the coffee van was also a winner. Would highly recommend this park to others. Hope to return one day!
Good facilities. Very busy. Can be a little noisy due to nearby cafe but all seems to shut down relatively early
